signals.headline: Solana Alpenglow Upgrade Passes Governance Vote with 98
signals.raw:
Solana Alpenglow Upgrade Passes Governance Vote with 98.27% Approval | YES | HIGH confidence**  
The Alpenglow upgrade, aimed at reducing Solana transaction finality to 100-150 milliseconds, was confirmed via a governance vote with 98.27% in favor, strengthening Solana's position as a high-speed layer-1 blockchain amid strong network activity like $57B DEX volume in March.[2]

signals.headline: Trump Orders US Navy Blockade of Strait of Hormuz on Iranian Ports After Failed Ceasefire Talks | NO | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Trump Orders US Navy Blockade of Strait of Hormuz on Iranian Ports After Failed Ceasefire Talks | NO | HIGH confidence**  
Ceasefire talks between the US and Iran stalled over the weekend in Pakistan (Islamabad), prompting President Trump to announce and implement a naval blockade starting April 13, 2026, targeting Iranian ships and vessels paying tolls to Iran.[1][2][3][5]

signals.headline: Bank of Korea holding benchmark interest rate at 2
signals.raw: Bank of Korea holding benchmark interest rate at 2.5% on April 10, 2026, citing inflation at ~2.2% (above 2% target) amid oil price surges and won weakness—widely anticipated, not new[3].
matched_markets: Will the price of Bitcoin be above $72,000 on April 16?

signals.headline: Annual inflation at 2
signals.raw: Annual inflation at 2.2% in March 2026 (up from 2.0% in Jan/Feb), below forecasts; February held at 2.0% on target[1][2].
matched_markets: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 2.7% and 2.9%?

signals.headline: Market turmoil from Middle East conflict (Iran Strait blockade), with won at 1,517/USD and KOSPI plunge, plus OECD raisi
signals.raw: Market turmoil from Middle East conflict (Iran Strait blockade), with won at 1,517/USD and KOSPI plunge, plus OECD raising 2026 inflation forecast to 2.7%—ongoing pressures, no fresh resolutions[4][5][7].

signals.raw: HIGH confidence** (search results cover up to early April 2026 with no post-April 10 events; prior data like January's 2.0% low is stale)[1][2].
